ucs.title: [LLM] Magic Hound (TA453) CharmPower / POWERSTAR PowerShell loader staging from cloud services
ucs.description: Hunts the CharmPower / POWERSTAR / GorjolEcho PowerShell loader chain Charming Kitten deploys after LNK-in-RAR spearphishing (e.g. the 2022/2023 Korg/POWERSTAR campaigns), where Office or shell parents spawn an obfuscated PowerShell that beacons to Dropbox, OneDrive, GitHub, or the Telegram Bot API for second-stage retrieval and C2.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=powershell.exe OR Processes.process_name=pwsh.exe) (Processes.process="*FromBase64String*" OR Processes.process="*-ec *" OR Processes.process="*-enc *" OR Processes.process="*IEX*" OR Processes.process="*Invoke-Expression*" OR Processes.process="*DownloadString*" OR Processes.process="*WebClient*") (Processes.process="*dropbox*" OR Processes.process="*api.telegram.org*" OR Processes.process="*onedrive*" OR Processes.process="*1drv.ms*" OR Processes.process="*googleapis.com*" OR Processes.process="*raw.githubusercontent.com*" OR Processes.process="*pastebin*") by host Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process Processes.process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where match(parent_process_name, "(?i)(winword|excel|powerpnt|outlook|wscript|cscript|mshta|rundll32|explorer)\\.exe$") OR like(parent_process_name, "%.lnk%")
| eval cloud_svc=case(match(process,"(?i)dropbox"),"dropbox", match(process,"(?i)telegram"),"telegram_bot", match(process,"(?i)onedrive|1drv"),"onedrive", match(process,"(?i)github"),"github", match(process,"(?i)googleapis"),"gcp", true(),"other")
| stats values(parent_process_name) as parents values(cloud_svc) as cloud_svcs values(process) as cmdlines min(firstTime) as firstTime max(lastTime) as lastTime by host, user
| convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime)
ucs.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("FromBase64String","Invoke-Expression","IEX(","-enc ","-ec ","DownloadString","WebClient","Net.WebRequest")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("dropbox","api.telegram.org","onedrive","1drv.ms","googleapis.com","raw.githubusercontent","pastebin")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName has_any ("WINWORD.EXE","EXCEL.EXE","POWERPNT.EXE","OUTLOOK.EXE","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","explorer.exe")
    or InitiatingProcessCommandLine has ".lnk"
| extend CloudSvc = case(
    ProcessCommandLine has "api.telegram.org", "telegram_bot",
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("dropbox"), "dropbox",
    ProcessCommandLine has_any ("onedrive","1drv.ms"), "onedrive",
    ProcessCommandLine has "raw.githubusercontent", "github",
    "other")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountUpn, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, CloudSvc
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any ("api.telegram.org","dropbox","1drv.ms","onedrive","raw.githubusercontent")
    | project DeviceName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, NetTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceName
| where abs(datetime_diff('second', Timestamp, NetTime)) < 600 or isnull(NetTime)
ucs.rationale: TA453/Charming Kitten's CharmPower (PWC/Check Point 2022) and POWERSTAR/GorjolEcho (Volexity/Proofpoint 2023) are the group's signature post-exploitation loaders. Both are PowerShell-based, launched from LNK-in-RAR or Office macro lures sent to journalists/academics/think-tanks, and uniquely abuse Dropbox, OneDrive, GitHub, and the Telegram Bot API for staging and bidirectional C2. The narrow comb

ucs.title: [LLM] Magic Hound (Charming Kitten / APT35) post-compromise mailbox hidden forwarding & inbox rule exfiltration
ucs.description: Detects Charming Kitten's hallmark M365/Exchange persistence pattern after a successful credential phish or password-spray of academics/journalists: creation of inbox rules or mailbox forwarders that silently copy or redirect mail to attacker-controlled addresses, often combined with rules that delete or hide the originals — the same TTP documented by PwC, Mandiant (UNC788), and Microsoft for Phosphorus/Mint Sandstorm.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Change.All_Changes where All_Changes.action=modified (All_Changes.object_category=mailbox OR All_Changes.command IN ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules","Set-Mailbox","Add-MailboxPermission","New-TransportRule")) by All_Changes.user All_Changes.src All_Changes.command All_Changes.object All_Changes.result _raw
| `drop_dm_object_name(All_Changes)`
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<fwd>(ForwardTo|ForwardingSmtpAddress|RedirectTo|DeliverToMailboxAndForward|ForwardAsAttachmentTo))\"?\s*[:=]\s*\"?(?<fwd_target>[^\",}]+)"
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<hide>(DeleteMessage|MoveToFolder|MarkAsRead|StopProcessingRules))\"?\s*[:=]\s*\"?(?<hide_val>true|Deleted Items|RSS Feeds|Junk|Archive|Conversation History)"
| where isnotnull(fwd_target) OR isnotnull(hide_val)
| eval external_fwd=if(match(fwd_target,"@") AND NOT match(fwd_target,"(?i)@(yourcorp\\.com|yourtenant\\.onmicrosoft\\.com)$"),1,0)
| join type=left user [
    | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.action=success Authentication.app="Office365*" by Authentication.user Authentication.src Authentication.signature
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Authentication)`
    | rename src as auth_src, signature as auth_sig
    | stats values(auth_src) as recent_logon_ips by user]
| where external_fwd=1 OR isnotnull(hide_val)
| table firstTime user src command fwd_target hide hide_val recent_logon_ips
ucs.kql:
let suspect_rules =
    CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType in ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules","Set-Mailbox","Add-MailboxPermission","New-TransportRule")
    | extend Params = parse_json(tostring(RawEventData.Parameters))
    | mv-apply p = Params on (
        extend pName = tostring(p.Name), pVal = tostring(p.Value)
      )
    | summarize ParamMap = make_bag(pack(pName, pVal)) by Timestamp, AccountId, AccountObjectId, ActionType, IPAddress, UserAgent, ReportId
    | extend Forward = tostring(ParamMap.ForwardTo), FwdSmtp = tostring(ParamMap.ForwardingSmtpAddress), Redirect = tostring(ParamMap.RedirectTo), MoveTo = tostring(ParamMap.MoveToFolder), Delete = tostring(ParamMap.DeleteMessage), StopProc = tostring(ParamMap.StopProcessingRules)
    | where isnotempty(Forward) or isnotempty(FwdSmtp) or isnotempty(Redirect) or MoveTo in~ ("Deleted Items","RSS Feeds","RSS Subscriptions","Junk Email","Archive","Conversation History") or Delete =~ "true"
    | extend FwdTarget = coalesce(Forward, FwdSmtp, Redirect)
    | extend ExternalForward = iff(FwdTarget has "@" and FwdTarget !endswith "@yourtenant.onmicrosoft.com" and FwdTarget !has "@yourcorp.com", true, false);
let risky_signins =
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | where RiskLevelDuringSignIn in ("medium","high") or RiskLevelAggregated in ("medium","high") or Country in ("IR","RU","VG","NL")
    | project SignInTime=Timestamp, AccountObjectId, SignInIP=IPAddress, Country, UserAgent, RiskLevelDuringSignIn;
suspect_rules
| join kind=leftouter risky_signins on AccountObjectId
| where ExternalForward or Delete =~ "true" or MoveTo in~ ("Deleted Items","RSS Feeds","RSS Subscriptions")
| where datetime_diff('hour', Timestamp, SignInTime) between (-72 .. 0) or isnull(SignInTime)
| project Timestamp, AccountId, ActionType, FwdTarget, ExternalForward, MoveTo, Delete, StopProc, IPAddress, UserAgent, SignInIP, Country, RiskLevelDuringSignIn
ucs.rationale: Hidden mailbox forwarding and 'RSS Feeds / Deleted Items' move-and-mark-read rules are a defining Charming Kitten / Mint Sandstorm post-compromise behaviour repeatedly attributed by PwC (2018), CERTFA (2018, 2021), Microsoft (Phosphorus advisories) and Mandiant (UNC788). The actor uses these rules to silently exfiltrate correspondence with academics, dissidents, and policy researchers after creden
